refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and manage a versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates with audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (persistence), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining the critical traceability and history requirements. High safety criticality demands explicit audit trail and baseline versioning to support safety case justification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) derived performance targets are traceable to documented worksite inputs via requirement matrix; (2) baseline/calibration history is immutable and timestamped; (3) all changes are logged with operator/system identity; (4) performance targets comply with worksite safety plan constraints.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with explicit traceability to source worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates with immutable audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Validate derived performance requirements against worksite safety plan and operational policies.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements database with derivation rules engine, traceability matrix, and versioned baseline management with audit logging.
DP-B: Distributed performance derivation across vehicle autonomy and teleoperation modules with centralized recording and history service.
DP-C: Template-based performance requirement generation with manual validation workflow, centralized storage, and change control process.
DP-D: Real-time performance requirement adaptation engine with continuous worksite input monitoring, baseline comparison, and automated calibration history.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance requirements database with derivation rules engine, traceability matrix, and versioned baseline management with audit logging.

potential_sources: Coupling to worksite input data sources (site conditions, task definitions) via Tasking interface—mitigated by explicit input schema.
Coupling to safety validation logic—mitigated by explicit safety constraint input and validation gate.
Coupling to vehicle/teleoperation performance feedback—mitigated by unidirectional Telemetry input for baseline comparison only.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it centralizes derivation logic and history management in a single logical domain (requirements engineering), with well-defined input/output interfaces. Decoupling is achieved by treating worksite inputs and performance feedback as external data sources rather than embedded logic.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context inputs are available in structured format (site conditions, task type, safety constraints) at system initialization and update.
ASSUMPTION: Derivation rules (mapping worksite context to performance targets) are stable and can be codified in a rules engine.
RISK: If worksite inputs are incomplete or inconsistent, derived requirements may be invalid; mitigation: explicit validation gate against safety plan.
RISK: If derivation rules are complex or domain-specific, rules engine may become difficult to maintain; mitigation: rules versioning and traceability.
RISK: High safety criticality demands immutable audit trail; performance impact of logging must be validated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) worksite input schema and validation rules; (2) derivation rule set and versioning strategy; (3) audit logging format and retention policy; (4) baseline comparison and calibration update triggers.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level performance requirements management capability, including derivation rules engine, traceability matrix, versioned baseline database, and audit logging. Owns the complete lifecycle of performance requirement derivation, recording, and management.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context inputs: site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints, operational policies. Used as source data for performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives vehicle/equipment performance feedback (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) for baseline comparison and calibration update triggers.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outputs validation alerts if derived performance requirements conflict with worksite safety plan or operational policies.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level container and owns the complete performance requirements management capability. Derivation rules engine, traceability matrix, baseline database, and audit logging are all system-level concerns. No diffusion risk.

Vehicle module is responsible for executing tasks and generating performance telemetry, not for deriving or managing performance requirements. Assigning DP-A to Vehicle would scatter requirements management logic across vehicle autonomy, creating high diffusion and maintenance burden.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operator control and situational awareness, not for system-level performance requirement derivation and management. Assigning DP-A to Teleoperation would couple operator interface to requirements engineering, creating high diffusion.

Safety Services owns safety validation and constraint enforcement, but not the complete performance requirements lifecycle (derivation, recording, history management). DP-A requires system-level ownership; Safety Services should be a consumer of derived requirements for validation only.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that must integrate worksite mission context, vehicle capabilities, and safety constraints; (2) traceability matrix and baseline history are system-level artifacts that must be accessible to all stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance); (3) audit logging and versioning are system-level governance concerns; (4) centralizing DP-A in NGHE Solution avoids diffusion of requirements management logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is not sufficiently mature or well-defined, assigning DP-A may overload it with complex requirements engineering logic. Mitigation: establish clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services.
RISK: If worksite input schema or derivation rules change frequently, NGHE Solution may require frequent updates. Mitigation: design rules engine with versioning and hot-reload capability.

RISK: If performance requirement derivation requires real-time feedback from vehicle/teleoperation, latency may impact safety-critical decisions. Mitigation: separate real-time performance monitoring (Vehicle/Teleoperation) from offline requirement derivation (NGHE Solution).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) establish explicit interface contracts for Tasking input, Telemetry input, and SafetyAlert output; (2) define ownership boundaries between NGHE Solution (requirement derivation/management) and Safety Services (requirement validation); (3) specify audit logging and baseline versioning as non-functional requirements for NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized requirements database with derivation rules engine, traceability matrix, and audit logging) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) derivation rules engine satisfies FR-005.1; (2) traceability matrix satisfies FR-005.2; (3) versioned baseline database with audit logging satisfies FR-005.3; (4) validation gate against safety plan satisfies FR-005.4. No independence violations detected; each sub-FR maps to distinct logical components within DP-A.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: Derivation rules engine logic is centralized in NGHE Solution; no diffusion across Vehicle/Teleoperation.
LOW RISK: Traceability matrix and baseline database are system-level artifacts owned by NGHE Solution; no duplication across modules.
LOW RISK: Audit logging is a cross-cutting concern but can be implemented as a service within NGHE Solution; no diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution provides appropriate containment for DP-A. All components of the DP (derivation rules, traceability, versioning, audit logging) are system-level concerns that should not be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and minimize coupling.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) requirement traceability matrix review (confirms FR-005.2); (2) baseline history audit log inspection (confirms FR-005.3); (3) derivation rule validation against worksite inputs (confirms FR-005.1); (4) safety constraint validation test (confirms FR-005.4). All verification methods are standard systems engineering practices.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the complete performance requirements management capability. Vehicle and Teleoperation provide input data (Tasking, Telemetry) but do not own requirement derivation or management. Safety Services validates derived requirements but does not own derivation logic. Ownership boundaries are explicit and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: Specify worksite input schema (site conditions, task type, safety constraints) as a formal interface specification for Tasking input.
Define derivation rule set and versioning strategy as a design specification for NGHE Solution.
Specify audit logging format, retention policy, and immutability guarantees as non-functional requirements.
Establish explicit interface contract between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for requirement validation.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (derivation, recording, management, validation), each of which requires further decomposition. DP-A (centralized requirements database with derivation rules engine) is sound and exhibits low coupling. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate with clear ownership boundaries and well-defined external interfaces. All gates pass. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for each sub-FR to specify detailed design parameters and implementation modules.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task type, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with explicit traceability to source worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain versioned history of performance baselines and calibration updates with immutable audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Validate derived performance requirements against worksite safety plan and operational policies.

assumptions: Worksite mission context inputs are available in structured format at system initialization and update.
Derivation rules (mapping worksite context to performance targets) are stable and can be codified in a rules engine.
Performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern owned by NGHE Solution, not distributed across Vehicle/Teleoperation.
Audit logging and baseline versioning are non-functional requirements that can be implemented within NGHE Solution without performance impact.
Safety Services module is available to validate derived requirements against worksite safety plan.
open_risks: If worksite inputs are incomplete or inconsistent, derived requirements may be invalid; mitigation: explicit validation gate against safety plan.
If derivation rules are complex or domain-specific, rules engine may become difficult to maintain; mitigation: rules versioning and traceability.
High safety criticality demands immutable audit trail; performance impact of logging must be validated in detailed design.

If performance requirement derivation requires real-time feedback from vehicle/teleoperation, latency may impact safety-critical decisions; mitigation: separate real-time monitoring from offline derivation.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of worksite input schema and validation rules.
Derivation rule set with traceability to performance targets and worksite context.
Audit logging format specification and immutability guarantees.
Interface contract between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for requirement validation.
Performance impact analysis of audit logging and baseline versioning.
Verification plan for traceability matrix, baseline history, and safety constraint validation.
